ai-engineer: domain knowledge changes frequently or corpus >100k docs
hosted llm with good prompting hits accuracy targets
narrow task with labeled data and well-defined output format
data cannot leave infrastructure (pii, regulatory, contractual)
results not needed within seconds (reports, nightly enrichment)
model is quantized, distilled, or onnx-optimized
data-analyst: aggregation, joins, filtering, window functions on structured data
statistical modeling, complex transforms, or visualization
question recurs on a regular cadence and audience is stable
data-engineer: consumers tolerate latency >15 min and volume fits scheduled windows
use case demands sub-minute freshness (fraud, real-time recs)
transforms run inside cloud warehouse (snowflake, bigquery, redshift) and data fits
data exceeds warehouse capacity or needs complex python (ml features, geospatial)
team already runs airflow and pain is manageable
greenfield and you want asset-based lineage
source schemas change frequently and you can't control upstream
data-scientist: goal is inference (understanding why, quantifying relationships)
goal is prediction and interpretability is secondary
logistic regression or linear model achieves acceptable performance
performance gap is significant and business impact justifies it
audience requires full transparency on every prediction
exploratory, one-off, or interactive stakeholder review
analysis will be re-run, scheduled, or integrated
model meets agreed success metric and tuning yields <1% improvement per iteration
llm-architect: knowledge base changes frequently or >100k docs
high-quality labeled data and narrow task
iteration speed matters more than cost
structured data or need exact match
scale >10m vectors and managed infra needed
task requires multi-step reasoning, tool use, or dynamic decisions
model must return json, function calls, or typed data
ml-engineer: tabular data and task fits in memory
feature reuse across models is likely
model performance degrades beyond agreed threshold
mlops-engineer: team already uses mlflow and it works
team needs rich visualization and collaborative comparison
org runs kubernetes and team has cluster expertise
prompt-engineer: structured output with known schema (json, function calls)
task requires specific format or reasoning style
search-specialist: sources mostly agree with minor variations
fullstack-developer: crud with simple queries then rest; else graphql when frontend needs flexible nested data
single team / single deploy then monolith; else services only when scaling or team boundaries demand it
real-time updates needed then websockets with pub/sub; elif simpler then sse or polling
validation rules exist then share via zod schema between api and frontend; never duplicate
feature modifies sensitive data then row-level security + api auth middleware + frontend route guard; else api-level protection minimum
database changes destructive then reversible migration with explicit up/down; else additive-only
business-analyst: the current process has logical flaws or missing steps
the process is sound but slow due to manual repetition
historical data exists and is reliable
feature falls outside defined project scope
product-manager: capability is core to competitive differentiation
adoption < 5% after two quarters with adequate awareness
usage declining qoq with no recovery path
quality perception critical for target segment
project-manager: requirements well-defined and unlikely to change
ux-researcher: question is "why do users do it?"
> 40% of participants fail primary task in usability testing
safety or accessibility concerns affect vulnerable users
research involves recording sessions (video, audio, screen capture)
working with vulnerable populations (minors, patients, employees being evaluated)
api-architect: operation is idempotent (get, put with full payload)
caller needs response to continue processing
database-architect: workload requires acid transactions, complex joins, or referential integrity
schema highly variable, document-oriented, or needs horizontal scaling with eventual consistency
services have distinct bounded contexts and independent deployment
table serves read-heavy dashboards or reports
additive (new table, new column with default)
destructive (column removal, type change, constraint tightening)
domain requires full audit trails, temporal queries, replay/reproject
graphql-architect: multiple teams owning distinct domain areas or schema > ~200 types
resolver fetches related entities in list context (e.g. `posts.author`)
field is scalar or always fetched individually
clients need real-time with reliable delivery
field always present when parent exists (e.g. `user.email`)
field can legitimately be absent or resolver can fail independently
change adds new types, fields, or enum values
postgres-pro: column used in equality/range queries with high selectivity
column stores jsonb with containment operators (`@>`, `?`, `?|`)
query filters on geometric or full-text data
table is append-only with time-ordered data
table > 100m rows and queries consistently filter on date/status
large table with uniformly distributed queries
specific large tables bloat while others fine
redis-specialist: durable event streaming with consumer groups
read-after-write consistency not critical and data source is bottleneck
write latency to primary store is bottleneck
some keys must never be evicted
dataset < 25 gb and need automatic failover
need horizontal sharding beyond single node
need persistence, replay, consumer groups, or exactly-once semantics
sql-pro: the query can be expressed in ansi sql-2016
optimization requires vendor-specific features (e.g., pg partial index, mysql `force index`, mssql `cross apply`)
column appears in where with high selectivity (< 5% of rows)
column used in full-text search
column is low-cardinality used only in combination
table is append-only, queries filter on monotonic column
both sides have indexes on join columns and result set is small
joining large tables without selective filters
one side is orders of magnitude smaller
ordered by indexed column and pages are sequential
user needs arbitrary page jumps (page 1, page 50)
large dataset with stable sort

adding not null column
renaming column
dropping column
adding index on large table
column can be null and appears in where
aggregating nullable column
joining on nullable column
query touches columns containing pii (names, emails, addresses, phone numbers, ips)
writing a migration that adds/modifies pii columns
building analytics queries on user data

team uses typescript/python and prefers imperative constructs
more than two teams or environments

source code developer edits on host

partial degradation affecting significant user segment (> 10%)
recent deploy correlates with symptom onset
not mitigated within 15 minutes for sev1
rollback is safe and fast (< 5 min)
rollback would cause data loss or break schema compatibility
the bug is in a database migration

plain manifests needing only per-environment patches
needs stable network identity + persistent storage ordering
external with l7 routing, tls, path-based rules

low-risk, reversible (dev env, feature branch, logs)
sre-engineer: cause-based metric proven to predict user impact

environments differ only in variable values
code-reviewer: all findings are minor or nits
any finding is critical (security flaw, data loss risk, incorrect business logic)
two or more major issues compound into systemic risk
incorrect behavior under realistic conditions or significant maintainability regression
untested code touches auth, payments, or user data
a known vulnerability pattern is introduced
the change breaks an existing public api contract without migration path
`grep` reveals the change broke an invariant elsewhere
you spot pre-existing debt unrelated to the pr
crypto usage, auth flows, or injection surfaces found
suspected o(n^2) path under production load
debugger: root cause is a typo, off-by-one, or wrong constant
root cause is a design flaw with large redesign blast radius
fix requires touching >3 files or changing an interface
regression with known "last known good" commit
commit history is shallow or squashed
an existing test covers the exact failure
no test exists for this path
root cause ambiguous after 3 hypothesis cycles
episode-orchestrator: all required fields present and unambiguous
task spans multiple domains (api + database + deployment)
two agent tasks have no input/output dependency
subagent error on critical step (data integrity, security, core logic)
failure on optional enrichment (formatting, docs generation)
request implicitly requires prerequisite work (e.g., refactoring before feature)
legacy-modernizer: legacy system is a monolith with identifiable domain boundaries
single module needs replacement but is deeply coupled
migrating data pipelines or business logic with correctness requirements
the team already knows the target stack
the legacy stack has a modern equivalent (e.g., java 8
the problem domain has shifted (e.g., batch
a module has high change frequency and clear boundaries
a module is stable and rarely touched
a module is the shared dependency for others
migration touches payment, auth, or data persistence
remaining legacy code is stable, well-tested, and rarely changed
maintenance cost of running two systems exceeds the cost of finishing
the team has moved on and the half-migrated state is the new normal

operation is fire-and-forget or triggers downstream workflows
eventual consistency acceptable and throughput matters
module has high change frequency and clear domain boundaries
deeply coupled with shared mutable state
two services share a database table
both need write access to same entity
crossing service boundaries with multi-step transaction
>5 services with cross-cutting concerns (mtls, retries, traffic shaping)

cache hit ratio high but latency unchanged
qa-expert: unit test coverage <80% on critical paths
feature touches payment, auth, or pii
feature is ui-only with no state changes

method is a trivial one-liner called only once
tests exist but are brittle or slow
conditional logic switches on type in 3+ places
object construction is complex with many optional params
complexity metrics meet target and tests pass

testing serialized output (api responses, rendered markup)
output contains timestamps, ids, or non-deterministic fields
diagram-architect: target needs visual editing by non-developers
diagram has >15 node types or complex layout
the relationship is obvious from context (e.g., `user
technical-writer: project has an established style guide
cpp-pro: resource has a single clear owner
ownership genuinely shared across independent lifetimes
project has an established error strategy
library code where callers may disable exceptions
open type set with runtime dispatch needed
performance-critical hot path with closed type set and benchmark proof
csharp-developer: dto, api response, or value object with no mutable state
mutable state, complex lifecycle, or needs inheritance
heavy filter pipelines, complex model binding, or odata
wrapping a completed value without suspension
expected business failure (validation, not found, conflict)
complex reporting joins or raw sql performance critical

internal to one package with one implementation

two modules have independent scaling, deployment cadence, or data ownership
complex queries (multi-join analytics, window functions, ctes)
backpressure, streaming, or sse/websocket fan-out needed

finite set of mutually exclusive states

subtypes share only a foreign key

single owner, heap needed (recursive types, trait objects)

needs compositional layout, attributed text, metal

mcp-developer: choosing a transport then: stdio for local cli/desktop apps
tool count ≤ 15 then single server with clear capability boundaries. if tool count > 15 then split by domain into focused servers behind a gateway
the server needs state across requests then use an external store (redis, sqlite, postgres) and keep the server process stateless. never use in-memory state in production

auditing an existing server for compliance then produce severity-rated findings (critical/high/medium/low) with spec section references and concrete remediation steps. if building a new server then im

deploying for local dev then stdio transport, `npm start` or `python -m`, no docker. if deploying to production then multi-stage docker build (<100mb node, <50mb python), health checks, graceful shutd
typescript then use zod for schema validation with `.describe()` on every field. if python then use json schema with `description` on every property
custom json-rpc error codes are used then verify they fall outside the reserved range (-32000 to -32099) or flag as spec violation
compliance-auditor: saas company handling customer data, primarily us market
payment card data touches any system
seeking international certification for enterprise sales
control is absent and data exposure is possible
control exists but evidence is incomplete or stale
control is implemented but not documented
control exceeds requirements but configuration could drift
control can be verified via code, config, logs, or infrastructure scan
control depends on human procedures (incident response, employee onboarding)
control is a hybrid (e.g., access reviews
critical severity and low remediation effort (<1 day)
multiple medium findings share a root cause
system is out of scope for the framework
penetration-tester: remote code execution or authentication bypass
data exfiltration possible but requires valid authentication
information disclosure without sensitive data (stack traces, internal ips, software versions)
theoretical vulnerability requiring unlikely preconditions (e.g., physical access, specific race condition window)
broad attack surface with limited time
narrow scope on a critical application
smart-contract-auditor: a protocol invariant is violated without direct fund loss
the issue is gas optimization or code quality
an optimization reduces readability of security-critical code (e.g., bit-packing access control flags, assembly in fund transfer logic)
the optimization targets a hot path called >1000x per block (batch processing, on-chain sorting, heavy loops in popular functions)
react-specialist: the component only renders data without interactivity then server component; else `'use client'` with minimum required hooks
an effect reads props/state that shouldn't trigger re-subscription then `useeffectevent()`; else keep in the dependency array
tab/panel content needs state preservation then `<activity mode="visible"|"hidden">`; else conditional render
a form submits to the server then actions api with `useactionstate` + `useformstatus`; else local `usestate`
the component uses `forwardref` then refactor to ref-as-prop (react 19)
a cached fetch in rsc should abort on cache expiry then attach `cachesignal` to abortcontroller
screenshot-ui-analyzer: the screenshot shows a recognizable component library (material icons, ant design patterns) then name it explicitly with confidence level; else describe the visual pattern and suggest the closest matc
interactive elements lack visible focus/hover states then flag as accessibility concern with wcag reference; else note the state patterns for implementation
the layout uses a clear grid system then specify column count and breakpoint hints; else describe spatial relationships qualitatively
color is the only differentiator for state (e.g., red = error) then flag as wcag 1.4.1 violation; else note the multi-signal approach
the screenshot contains truncated text or overflow indicators then note responsive behavior requirements; else assume content fits
ui-designer: utility-first development with class constraints then tailwind with configured theme; elif co-located dynamic styles then css-in-js (vanilla-extract for zero-runtime); else css modules with shared tok
strong custom visual identity then headless components (radix, headless ui) with custom styling; elif speed > uniqueness then shadcn/ui or mui. pick one, commit
runtime theming needed (dark mode toggle, white-labeling) then css custom properties; else js constants with build-time transformer
product has specific brand requirements then thin design system on headless components; elif internal tool or mvp then existing library, customize token layer only
dark mode then semantic token re-mapping
vue-expert: legacy options api codebase then maintain in existing components, composition api in new ones; else `<script setup>` exclusively
state shared across non parent-child components then pinia; elif scoped to a subtree then `provide`/`inject` with composable; else local `ref`
static content + seo then ssg with `nuxi generate`; elif dynamic + seo then ssr with caching; else spa mode
logic reused across multiple components then extract into composable; else keep inline
programmatic render or `.ts` without template then `definecomponent`; else `<script setup>`
